question_text: A student uses a crowbar to lift a heavy rock. The crowbar is a lever with the pivot (fulcrum) near the rock. Which statement correctly describes what happens to the effort force needed?

options.label: A
options.text: The effort force needed is larger than the load force because the lever always increases the force.

options.label: B
options.text: The effort force needed is smaller than the load force because the effort distance from the pivot is much larger than the load distance.

options.label: C
options.text: The effort force needed is the same as the load force because force cannot change.

options.label: D
options.text: The effort force needed is smaller than the load force because levers reduce all forces by half.

explanation: A lever works by the principle of moments: effort force × effort distance = load force × load distance. When the effort distance is much larger than the load distance, a smaller effort force can balance a larger load force. This is why a crowbar makes lifting heavy objects easier.
